message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Castelli's Ristorante (Palm Desert)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 748 reviews, Tripadvisor 577 reviews, Restaurantji aggregates ~860; Google review count [UNVERIFIED] — search summary cited 4.5 stars but exact count not captured
- Sentiment: mostly positive with mixed recent signals — strong on nostalgia, food, piano-bar ambiance; recurring complaints about rushed turn-times, table-pacing, and inconsistency on certain dishes (osso buco, seafood) in recent reviews
- Longevity: Operating since 1988 (Memorial Day) at 73098 Hwy 111, Palm Desert; founded by brothers John and Michael Castelli after relocating from San Clemente. Michael Castelli died Sept 2023 per KESQ; ownership continuity post-2023 not explicitly clarified in sources but business is operating into 2026 with Chef Victor Martin cited as longtime kitchen lead
- Credentials: ["Zagat-rated among America's top Italian restaurants (cited by Palm Springs Life — exact year/edition [UNVERIFIED])","Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' Best Italian (cited as 2018 by listing aggregator; 2021 PSL Italian feature inaccessible to verify winner)","Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice","Featured in Palm Springs Life, Coachella Magazine, Golfing Palm Springs Magazine","Tripadvisor rank #12 of 247 Palm Desert restaurants"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for 'best Italian Palm Desert' (consistently named alongside La Spiga, Mamma Gina, Mimmo's, Trattoria Mezzomondo, Mario's) and for 'old-school Italian Coachella Valley' (named first in that category alongside Johnny Costa's and Mario's). Does NOT surface in 'date night Palm Desert romantic Italian' queries — Mimmo's, Backstreet Bistro, Mamma Gina dominate that frame instead. Absent from 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' general lists unless query is cuisine-scoped.
- Site structured data: Own site castellis.cc returned HTTP 403 to automated fetch — suggests bot-blocking / no agent-friendly access path. Cannot confirm schema.org markup, JSON-LD, machine-readable hours, or menu structure. This is a meaningful agent-visibility gap: the brand's authoritative source is closed to crawlers, forcing agents to rely on third-party aggregators (Yelp, OpenTable, Tripadvisor, Visit Greater Palm Springs) for canonical data.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, I found information about Michael Castelli's obituary:

Michael Joseph Castelli passed away on September 16, 2023, at the age of 66. He was born on August 26, 1957, in St. Louis, Missouri.

Michael and his brother John were long-time proprietors of Castelli's Ristorante in Palm Desert. For 35 years, Michael was the heart and soul of Castelli's Ristorante, where he shared his love for great food and hospitality with the community.

A memorial service in honor of Michael Joseph Castelli was held on October 4, 2023, at 1:00 PM at St. Francis of Assisi Catholic Church in La Quinta, CA. Following the ceremony, a Celebration of Life was held at Castelli's Restaurant.

He is survived by his two children, daughter Brittany Leger, 39, and son Michael Joseph Castelli Jr., 15.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Castelli's Ristorante in Palm Desert:

## History and Founding

Co-owner John Castelli states: "We brought Castelli's Ristorante to the Coachella Valley in 1988, along with our original recipes." More specifically, the restaurant first opened on Memorial Day in 1988.

## The Castelli Brothers

St. Louis–born brothers Michael and John Castelli moved to the desert three decades ago, taking a chance on a modest eatery on Highway 111 where they planned to share their favorite Old World Italian family recipes with guests. John and Michael Castelli moved their family and restaurant from San Clemente to the desert, bringing with them the traditions of Italian dining.

## Success and Legacy

For 35 years, Castelli's Ristorante has been the quintessential destination for authentic Italian cuisine in the Coachella Valley. Zagat rated Castelli's among America's top 1,000 Italian restaurants, and Palm Springs Life readers voted it Best of the Best in Palm Desert.

The restaurant is known for its signature dishes like fettuccine Alfredo and osso bucco, which have remained on the menu since the early days, as well as its old-world Italian atmosphere with live piano entertainment.

content.text: Note: Tripadvisor rank may be #14 (latest) vs claimed #12 — minor difference. Let me verify the Tripadvisor review count and check the Coachella Magazine source for credentials.

input.prompt: What is the exact total review count, star rating, and current rank among Palm Desert restaurants? Is Travelers' Choice award shown?
What awards, credentials, history, founding year, and ownership details are mentioned for Castelli's Ristorante?

content.content:
# Castelli's Restaurant Details

**Review Count & Rating:** The restaurant has "4.2 of 5 bubbles" with "(577 reviews)"

**Current Ranking:** "#12 of 247 Restaurants in Palm Desert"

**Travelers' Choice Award:** Yes, the restaurant displays a Travelers' Choice award. According to the page, this award goes to establishments that "consistently earn great reviews from travelers and are ranked within the top 10% of properties on Tripadvisor."

# Castelli's Ristorante: Awards, Credentials, and History

**Founding & Ownership:**
The restaurant was established in 1988 by co-owners John and Michael Castelli, who relocated the business from San Clemente to the Coachella Valley, bringing their original family recipes with them.

**Awards & Recognition:**
The article references "award-winning dishes" and notes that Castelli's earned the distinction of being called "The King of Fettuccine" due to customer popularity of their Fettuccine Alfredo. However, no specific awards or formal credentials are detailed in the piece.

**Notable Credentials:**
- 35 years of operation in the Coachella Valley
- Recognition as a "beloved fine dining staple"
- Current leadership under Chef Victor Martin, who has nearly 24 years in the culinary industry

**Historical Significance:**
The restaurant has hosted high-profile guests including President Gerald Ford, establishing what became their famous "Celebrity Room" displaying photographs of notable diners including celebrities and politicians.

content.text: Tripadvisor confirms #12, 577 reviews, Travelers' Choice. Let me verify Yelp count and the Palm Springs Life "Best of the Best" claim more precisely.

content.content:

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Castelli's in Palm Desert and Chef Victor Martin:

Chef Victor Martin is at the helm of this iconic restaurant, Castelli's Ristorante in Palm Desert, CA. Chef Martin has been in the industry for almost 24 years, working his way up from washing dishes and learning every step of the process, with his mission being to "make it better" while maintaining the restaurant's traditions.

Chef Martin emphasizes that Castelli's doesn't cut corners, with non-negotiable standards—he would rather remove a dish from the menu than serve something that doesn't meet their quality. The restaurant is renowned for its exceptional offerings, from prime beef, veal, lamb, and seafood to signature pasta dishes like their famed Fettuccine Alfredo.

The restaurant is located at 73098 Highway 111, Palm Desert, CA 92260, and for 35 years, Castelli's Ristorante has been the quintessential destination for authentic Italian cuisine in the Coachella Valley.

content.text:
I have enough verification. Yelp listing shows "748 Reviews" confirmed. Tripadvisor 577 reviews, #12 of 247, Travelers' Choice confirmed. Founding 1988, brothers John & Michael, San Clemente relocation confirmed. Michael's death Sept 16, 2023 confirmed via KESQ/obituaries. Chef Victor Martin confirmed.

The "Best of the Best" Palm Springs Life year-by-year (2017-2023) appeared in search snippet but the source page returned 403, so I cannot fully verify each year. Zagat "top 1,000 Italian restaurants" claim appears widely sourced but is essentially a self-cited/Zagat-historic claim — Zagat ceased traditional guide publishing years ago, so this is historical (likely accurate but undated).

confirms: Operating since 1988 at 73098 Hwy 111, Palm Desert — confirmed by Coachella Magazine and multiple sources
Founded by brothers John and Michael Castelli, relocated from San Clemente — confirmed by Coachella Magazine and Palm Springs Life snippet
Michael Castelli died Sept 16, 2023 — confirmed by KESQ and Forest Lawn obituary (age 66, born Aug 26 1957 in St. Louis)
Chef Victor Martin as longtime kitchen lead (~24 years in industry) — confirmed by Coachella Magazine and Castelli's social media
Tripadvisor: 577 reviews, rank #12 of 247 Palm Desert restaurants, Travelers' Choice award — directly verified on Tripadvisor page
Yelp: 748 reviews — confirmed via Yelp listing title (4.2 stars on Tripadvisor; Yelp rating not separately confirmed here)
Zagat-rated among America's top 1,000 Italian restaurants — widely cited across sources (note: historical, undated; Zagat print guides discontinued)
Featured in Palm Springs Life and Coachella Magazine — confirmed via direct articles
flagged_as_unverified: Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' Best Italian specific year 2018 — search snippet claimed 2017-2023 consecutive wins, but the PSL article returned HTTP 403 and could not be directly verified; the claim as stated (2018) is plausible but not independently confirmed at the source
Restaurantji aggregate of ~860 reviews — not independently verified in this pass
Featured in Golfing Palm Springs Magazine — not independently verified in this pass

Ownership continuity post-Michael Castelli's 2023 death — sources confirm John Castelli as surviving co-owner and the business is operating, but no source explicitly details the post-2023 ownership/succession structure
Zagat citation: the specific edition/year of the 'top 1,000 Italian' rating is not pinned down (Zagat guides have been discontinued in print, so this is a legacy claim repeated by the restaurant)
input.notes: Core history and credentials check out under independent verification. Founding (1988, brothers John/Michael, San Clemente relocation), Michael Castelli's death (Sept 16, 2023), Chef Victor Martin, Tripadvisor #12/247 with 577 reviews and Travelers' Choice, and Yelp 748 reviews all confirmed via primary or near-primary sources. Minor note: Tripadvisor star rating shows 4.2 (not previously stated). The PSL 'Best of the Best' claim is reasonable but the specific year (2018) couldn't be source-verified due to 403; a search snippet suggests multi-year wins 2017-2023 but that needs treating with caution since snippets are summaries, not source text. Zagat rating is a legacy/undated claim — true historically but not currently certifiable. No James Beard, Michelin, or Food Network claims were made (correctly so — none found). No contradictions surfaced.
